system_posture.summary: Current admitted evidence is strong enough to drive risk auditing and action prioritization, but boundary and provenance limits still block benchmark-level claims.

black_box.quality_cost: 100 public samples per split; DDIM step30; artifact-mainline ready; cuda runtime
black_box.boundary: controlled / public-subset / proxy-shadow-member / risk-exists; not a final exploit or full real-world benchmark

gray_box_baseline.quality_cost: attack_num=30; interval=10; batch_size=8; 512 samples per split; single GPU serial; adaptive repeats=3; wall-clock=212.993833s
gray_box_baseline.note: current gray-box baseline reference; score is derived from epsilon-trajectory consistency under the official PIA DDPM attacker
gray_box_baseline.boundary: workspace-verified + adaptive-reviewed + paper-alignment blocked by checkpoint/source provenance

gray_box_defended.quality_cost: attack_num=30; interval=10; batch_size=8; 512 samples per split; single GPU serial; adaptive repeats=3; wall-clock=223.128438s
gray_box_defended.note: current gray-box defended main result after repeated-query review; all_steps remains stronger than late_steps_only while keeping surrogate quality drift small
gray_box_defended.boundary: workspace-verified + adaptive-reviewed + paper-alignment blocked by checkpoint/source provenance

white_box_attack.note: current white-box attack main evidence promoted from epoch300 rerun1 under the same 1k-3shadow protocol
white_box_attack.boundary: admitted white-box bridge / risk upper-bound; not a final paper-level benchmark

white_box_defended.boundary: admitted white-box bridge / defended comparator; not a final paper-level benchmark

gray-box.quality_cost: t_sec=100; k=10; batch_size=64; 25000 samples per split; single GPU serial; stat line plus NNS auxiliary head
gray-box.note: real SecMI full-split GPU run on imported CIFAR10 checkpoint bundle; NNS auxiliary head reaches AUC 0.946286 on the same rung
gray-box.boundary: workspace-verified + full local split execution; still not automatically a paper-faithful benchmark claim

white-box.note: defended reference rung kept for comparison
white-box.boundary: admitted white-box bridge / defended comparator; not a final paper-level benchmark

suggestions.title: Assume privileged access remains catastrophic
suggestions.why: GSA 1k-3shadow reaches auc=0.998192 / asr=0.9895, which is a white-box upper-bound failure signal.
suggestions.action: Keep high-privilege model access behind explicit review and use white-box results as the upper-bound audit line rather than a product-facing KPI.
suggestions.boundary: admitted white-box bridge / risk upper-bound; not a final paper-level benchmark

suggestions.title: Treat PIA baseline as the main optimization pressure
suggestions.why: PIA GPU512 baseline remains the strongest attack-defense mainline with auc=0.841339 / asr=0.786133.
suggestions.action: Use this baseline as the canonical attack target for further GPU tuning and compare every candidate defense against the same adaptive-reviewed PIA path.
suggestions.boundary: workspace-verified + adaptive-reviewed + paper-alignment blocked by checkpoint/source provenance

suggestions.title: Keep stochastic-dropout on the defended critical path
suggestions.why: provisional G-1 = stochastic-dropout (all_steps) lowers the admitted gray-box metrics while preserving the current runtime path.
suggestions.action: Continue tuning inference-time stochastic dropout against the admitted PIA baseline and reject candidates that do not beat the current defended pairing on both effect and cost.
suggestions.boundary: workspace-verified + adaptive-reviewed + paper-alignment blocked by checkpoint/source provenance

suggestions.title: Keep recon as the ingress risk probe
suggestions.why: recon DDIM public-100 step30 still shows externally observable leakage risk at auc=0.849 / asr=0.51.
suggestions.action: Keep black-box auditing live on controlled/public-subset bundles and attach rate-limit, logging, and review hooks before claiming deployment readiness.
suggestions.boundary: controlled / public-subset / proxy-shadow-member / risk-exists; not a final exploit or full real-world benchmark

suggestions.title: Use W-1 as the defended comparator, not the final answer
suggestions.why: W-1 strong-v3 full-scale is currently the best defended white-box comparator in the admitted table.
suggestions.action: Use this defended rung as the current white-box reduction reference while keeping benchmark claims frozen at the bridge level.
suggestions.boundary: admitted white-box bridge / defended comparator; not a final paper-level benchmark

deployment_blockers.title: Gray-box mainline remains bounded by provenance
deployment_blockers.detail: workspace-verified + adaptive-reviewed + paper-alignment blocked by checkpoint/source provenance
deployment_blockers.required_before_promotion: Do not describe PIA as paper-faithful or benchmark-ready until checkpoint/source provenance closes.

deployment_blockers.title: White-box attack is an upper-bound audit line
deployment_blockers.detail: admitted white-box bridge / risk upper-bound; not a final paper-level benchmark
deployment_blockers.required_before_promotion: Use GSA as privileged-risk evidence only; do not present it as a normal deployment KPI.

deployment_blockers.title: W-1 remains a defended comparator, not a final benchmark
deployment_blockers.detail: admitted white-box bridge / defended comparator; not a final paper-level benchmark
deployment_blockers.required_before_promotion: Keep W-1 as the current defended bridge until a stronger admitted white-box defense path exists.

deployment_blockers.title: Black-box recon evidence is still subset-bounded
deployment_blockers.detail: controlled / public-subset / proxy-shadow-member / risk-exists; not a final exploit or full real-world benchmark
deployment_blockers.required_before_promotion: Keep claims limited to controlled public-subset leakage risk, not generalized internet exploitability.

gpu_next_action.why: This rung gives the best positive defense movement per unit wall clock among the currently paired PIA summaries.

dropout_schedule_recommendation.why: all_steps still delivers the stronger privacy drop on the freshest same-rung ablation trio.

auditor.primary_focus: Confirm risk exists, keep boundary language intact, and escalate high-risk tracks before deployment language appears.

operator.primary_focus: Treat recon as ingress detection, keep privileged access gated, and prefer stochastic-dropout on the gray-box defended path.

research.primary_focus: Keep tuning on the admitted PIA defended line without reopening the frozen mainline.

notes: This report is built from the admitted attack-defense table plus current PIA paired runtime summaries.
Suggestions are machine-generated heuristics for audit and tuning prioritization; they do not override provenance or benchmark boundaries.
